description: Chatbot Logo setting uses WordPress media uploader instead of a plain URL text field. Admin clicks 'Upload' button, selects/uploads an image via WP media library, and the resulting attachment URL is stored in the chatbot_logo setting. Frontend rendering unchanged.
steps: Open chatbot admin → Appearance tab
Verify 'Chatbot Logo' field shows an upload button (not a URL text input)
Click upload — verify WordPress media library modal opens
Select or upload an image — verify the selected image URL populates the setting
Verify a thumbnail preview of the selected logo appears next to the upload button
Verify a 'Remove' button appears when a logo is set
Click Remove — verify the logo preview disappears and the setting is cleared
Save settings — verify the logo URL persists in the database
Verify the frontend widget displays the uploaded logo correctly

description: Handoff to Human flow fails after collecting name and email — bot reports an error to the user, no support request row is created in wpaic_support_requests, and no admin email is sent. The full handoff flow (collect info → call create_handoff_request tool → save to DB → send email → confirm to user) must complete without errors.
steps: Enable 'Handoff to Human' in admin → Engagement tab
Open the chat widget and ask to speak to a human
Provide name and email when prompted by the bot
Verify the bot does NOT display an error message
Verify a new row exists in wpaic_support_requests table with correct name, email, and transcript
Verify the admin receives an email notification with customer details and conversation summary
Verify the bot confirms the request was submitted (e.g. 'Our team will contact you shortly')
Verify the support request appears in admin → Support Requests page with status 'new'

description: Handoff to Human allows admin to configure which optional contact fields the bot collects before submitting the request. Fields are presented as selectable chips in admin UI. Available optional fields: phone number, company, order number, request message. Name and email are always required and cannot be deselected. No optional fields selected by default.
steps: Open chatbot admin → Engagement tab with Handoff enabled
Verify a 'Required Fields' chip-select UI appears below the handoff toggle
Verify 'Name' and 'Email' chips are always present and visually locked (cannot be deselected)
Verify optional chips (phone number, company, order number, request message) are deselected by default
Select additional chips (e.g. phone number, request message) and save
Verify saved selections persist after page reload
Open chat widget and request to speak to a human
Verify the bot asks for name, email, and only the selected optional fields
Provide all requested info — verify the support request is created with all fields stored
Deselect 'Phone Number', save, and test again — verify bot no longer asks for phone
Verify the create_handoff_request tool schema parameters match the selected fields

description: Chatbot Logo media uploader allows selecting any file type (PDFs, videos, etc.). The WP media modal must restrict selectable files to web-safe image formats only (JPEG, PNG, GIF, WebP, SVG). Additionally, the logo preview in the admin Appearance tab can render at full size if the source image is large — cap the admin preview height to match the frontend widget constraint (~32px).
steps: Open chatbot admin → Appearance tab
Click 'Upload Logo' — verify WP media library opens
Verify non-image files (PDFs, videos, docs) are either hidden or greyed out / unselectable
Verify only image files (JPEG, PNG, GIF, WebP, SVG) can be selected
Select a very large image (e.g. 2000×2000px) as the logo
Verify the admin preview thumbnail does not exceed ~32px in height
Save and reload — verify the preview remains constrained
Open the frontend widget — verify the logo displays at the same constrained 32px max-height

description: Sending a chat message causes the text input to lose focus. After submitting a message (Enter or Send button), the input field must retain keyboard focus so the user can immediately type the next message without clicking the field again.
steps: Open the chat widget on the frontend
Click the text input to focus it
Type a message and press Enter to send
Verify the text input remains focused (cursor blinking in the field) after the message is sent
Type another message immediately without clicking the input — verify it works
Send a message using the Send button — verify input re-focuses after send
Verify focus is retained even while the bot response is streaming

description: Chat text input supports multiline entry. Pressing Shift+Enter inserts a newline instead of sending. The input field (currently a single-line <input>) must become a <textarea> that auto-grows in height as lines are added, up to a maximum of ~5-6 visible lines. Beyond that, the textarea becomes scrollable. Pressing Enter alone (without Shift) still sends the message.
steps: Open the chat widget on the frontend
Verify the input renders as a textarea (not a single-line input)
Type text and press Shift+Enter — verify a new line is inserted and the message is NOT sent
Verify the textarea grows taller to accommodate 2, 3, 4, 5 lines
Verify the textarea stops growing after ~5-6 lines and becomes vertically scrollable
Type text across multiple lines and press Enter (without Shift) — verify the message is sent
Verify the sent message preserves line breaks when displayed in the chat
Verify the textarea resets to single-line height after sending
Verify the textarea is still disabled during bot response streaming

description: Clicking 'Clear chat' in the frontend widget saves the current conversation as a completed chat log entry before clearing. Currently, clearing the chat discards session messages without persisting them. After this change: the frontend calls an endpoint to finalize/close the current conversation, then starts a fresh session. The cleared conversation appears in admin Chat Logs as a separate entry.
steps: Open the chat widget and send 3+ messages, receiving bot responses
Note the current session's conversation content
Click the 'Clear chat' button (↺) in the chat header
Verify the chat clears and shows the greeting/welcome state
Send a new message — verify it starts a fresh conversation (new session ID)
Open admin → Chat Logs
Verify the previous conversation (before clear) appears as its own log entry with correct message count
Verify the new conversation (after clear) is tracked as a separate entry
Verify the cleared conversation's messages are viewable via the 'View' button in Chat Logs

description: Remove the 'ID', 'User', and 'Session' columns from the Chat Logs table in admin. These fields add clutter without providing actionable information to the admin. The remaining columns (Messages, Started, Last Activity, Actions) are sufficient.
steps: Open admin → Chat Logs page
Verify the table does NOT show an 'ID' column
Verify the table does NOT show a 'User' column
Verify the table does NOT show a 'Session' column
Verify the table still shows: Messages, Started, Last Activity, and Actions columns
Verify the 'View' and 'Delete' action buttons still work correctly
Verify pagination still works with the reduced column set
